name: stranded-dirty-files
question: Which dirty files in this project have no live owner and predate the most recent finalize commit?

schema.has_stranded: boolean (true iff count > 0; false when count == 0 OR count == -1)
schema.count: integer (number of stranded files; -1 means detection skipped — too many dirty files >2000)
schema.oldest_mtime: string|null (ISO-8601 UTC mtime of the oldest stranded file; null when count == 0 OR detection skipped)
schema.sample: array (capped at 10) of {path: string, mtime: ISO-8601 UTC, age_days: int}
schema.live_peer_count: integer (number of live peers consulted for ownership; 0 when registry missing)
schema.last_finalize_at: string|null (ISO-8601 UTC commit timestamp of HEAD; null when not in a git repo OR no commits)
schema.briefing: string (empty when no stranded files; populated when count > 0 OR count == -1)

_briefingForms.no_stranded: ""  (empty -> session-start hook suppresses the line, parallel to stale-ltads-state)
_briefingForms.small_stranded_<50: "stranded-dirty-files: <N> files (oldest <D> days; no live owner) — run /0-uldf-finalize --include-stranded for cleanup"
_briefingForms.large_stranded_>=50: "stranded-dirty-files: <N> files (oldest <D> days) — significant accumulation; see /0-uldf-oracle stranded-dirty-files for full sample"
_briefingForms.detection_skipped: "stranded-dirty-files: detection skipped — too many dirty files (>2000); run /0-uldf-finalize --include-stranded for full audit"

scope_guard.rationale: Above 2000 dirty files, mtime stat + per-file ownership probe blows the briefing budget. Oracle returns count: -1 sentinel; user runs /0-uldf-finalize --include-stranded for the full audit.

consultation.typical_sessions_using: every (cheap; gracefully absent on clean projects); also consumed by /0-uldf-finalize --include-stranded flag handling

fallback: Run `git status --porcelain` to enumerate dirty files. Read `git log -1 --format=%aI HEAD` for last_finalize_at. For each dirty file, stat mtime and compare against last_finalize_at; if older AND no live peer claims it (per .claude/collaboration/active-sessions.json `dirtyFiles[]` registry field, defaulting to no-claim), it is a strand candidate. Cap sample at 10 entries; report full count separately. Above 2000 dirty files, skip detection.

detectionContract.summary: Stranded = (file in `git status --porcelain` dirty set) AND (mtime < HEAD's commit timestamp) AND (no live peer claims via dirtyFiles[] registry field).

ownership.source: .claude/collaboration/active-sessions.json sessions[] entries where status=='active' AND PID alive AND workDir matches this project root
ownership.field: dirtyFiles[] (forward-compatible — defaults to empty array; until peers publish ownership, default = 'no live peer' which is the correct default-to-strand behavior)

boundary.source: git log -1 --format=%aI HEAD (commit timestamp of HEAD; the most-recent-finalize boundary per spec acceptance §3)
boundary.rationale: The last committed timestamp is the project's effective finalize boundary; refined detection (parsing commit messages for /0-uldf-finalize markers) is deferred to Phase 1.7.1 if the simple boundary proves insufficient.

detectionContract.frozenSchema: The output schema is FROZEN at first commit. Programmatic consumers (/0-uldf-finalize --include-stranded partition logic; session-start hook briefing-line emission) read this shape. Changes require an explicit decision-channel entry plus LD approval — never silent.

design_lineage: stale-ltads-state (CSI-14) — same briefing-line silence pattern, same always-fresh + ~250ms budget shape
dispatchable-sessions (CSI-05) — registry-aware project-state oracle skeleton (parser fallback chain, JSON output)
workspace-shared-repos (SHARED-CSI-01) — frozen-schema discipline + Set 1/Set 2 partition mirroring (consumed by --include-stranded flag wiring per FINALIZE-04 acceptance #3)
provenance.note: Visibility-only oracle (CSI Phase 1.7). Never mutates state; cleanup is driven by /0-uldf-finalize --include-stranded (FINALIZE-04, separate spec entry — same Arc 2).
